sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: 2025년 3분기 매출 129억 700만 달러를 기록하며 정부 지출 효율화 정책에 따른 계약 지연 및 해지 리스크에 대응 중입니다.

keyPoints.text: 미국 정부의 지출 효율화 기구(DOGE) 활동으로 인해 연방 정부의 인력 감축, 채용 동결 및 계약 수정·해지가 발생하고 있습니다.
IT 서비스 부문을 중심으로 정부 기관의 우선순위 변경에 따른 계약 지연과 해지가 사업 성과에 영향을 미치고 있습니다.
2025년 9개월간 영업활동 현금흐름은 35억 5,900만 달러로, 전년 동기 19억 5,200만 달러 대비 크게 개선되었습니다.

riskChanges.text: 정부 지출 효율화 정책(DOGE)에 따른 계약 지연 및 해지 가능성이 사업 불확실성을 높이고 있습니다.
미국 정부의 새로운 관세 정책 시행이 향후 공급망 및 비용 구조에 미칠 영향이 잠재적 리스크로 작용하고 있습니다.

businessChanges.text: 항공우주 부문의 매출은 신규 항공기 인도 수량과 인도 시점의 생산 단위당 평균 비용에 따라 변동성을 보입니다.
2025년 9개월간 영업활동 현금흐름 증가는 주로 순이익 성장에 기인하며, 전년 대비 운전자본 부담이 완화되었습니다.

financialHighlights.text: 당분기 매출 129억 700만 달러, 영업이익 13억 3,100만 달러, 순이익 10억 5,900만 달러를 달성했습니다.
총자산 575억 9,900만 달러를 보유하고 있으며, 현금 및 현금성 자산은 25억 2,000만 달러 수준입니다.

watchItems.text: 정부 지출 효율화 정책이 IT 서비스 외 타 방산 부문으로 확대될 경우, 수주 잔고의 질적 변화와 매출 성장세 둔화 여부를 면밀히 모니터링해야 합니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: Quarter ended 2025-09-28 revenue = 129억 700만 달러 (USD 12.907B; 12,907 million), XBRL range 2025-06-30..2025-09-28.
Quarter ended 2025-09-28 operating income = 13억 3,100만 달러 (USD 1.331B; 1,331 million), XBRL range 2025-06-30..2025-09-28.
Quarter ended 2025-09-28 net income = 10억 5,900만 달러 (USD 1.059B; 1,059 million), XBRL range 2025-06-30..2025-09-28.
Quarter ended 2025-09-28 total assets = 575억 9,900만 달러 (USD 57.599B; 57,599 million), XBRL range instant..2025-09-28.
Quarter ended 2025-09-28 cash and cash equivalents = 25억 2,000만 달러 (USD 2.52B; 2,520 million), XBRL range instant..2025-09-28.

Results of Operations section that follows, we have included our current outlook for each operating segment. This outlook reflects management's judgment in light of the uncertainties due to the government shutdown. In 2025, the administration has taken steps to address federal spending, including forming the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) to assist in this process. Thus far, the directives of the administration and actions of the DOGE have resulted in federal government staff reductions, hiring freezes, contract modifications and terminations, and delays in contract awards. We have experienced some award delays and contract terminations as a result of these actions as well as changes in agency priorities, largely within our IT services business. In addition, the administration has implemented new tariffs as part of U.S. trade policy. The duration and extent of the tariffs and

Revenue associated with the segment’s services businesses is recognized as work progresses or upon delivery of services. Fluctuations in revenue from period to period result from the number and mix of new aircraft deliveries, and the level and type of aircraft services performed during the period. The majority of the Aerospace segment’s operating costs relates to new aircraft production on firm orders and consists of labor, material, subcontractor and overhead costs. The costs are accumulated in production lots, recorded in inventory and recognized as operating costs at aircraft delivery based on the estimated average unit cost in a production lot. While changes in the estimated average unit cost for a production lot impact the level of operating costs, the amount of operating costs reported in a given period is based largely on the number and type of aircraft delivered. Operating costs

Cash Flows in Part I, Item 1: Nine Months Ended September 28, 2025 September 29, 2024 Net cash provided by operating activities $ 3,559 $ 1,952 Net cash used by investing activities (422) (588) Net cash used by financing activities (2,306) (1,173) OPERATING ACTIVITIES Cash provided by operating activities was $3.6 billion in the first nine months of 2025 compared with $2 billion in the same period in 2024. The primary driver of cash flows in both periods was net earnings. Cash flows in 2024 were affected negatively by growth in operating working capital, particularly driven by timing in our Aerospace and Combat Systems segments.
